items.name: Filesystem MCP
items.description: The MCP project's reference server for safe file work: reading, writing, and searching within explicitly allowed directories. The server limits its own permissions to a list of paths.
items.useFor: Anyone who wants to give Claude access to a specific folder (say, "Documents") without opening the whole disk: list the allowed directories right in the launch command.
items.install: claude mcp add filesystem -- npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem /path/to/directory

items.name: Fetch MCP
items.description: A reference server: fetches web pages and converts them into a model-friendly format (markdown). Shows how to prepare raw web data for an LLM.
items.useFor: When you need Claude to read pages by URL in environments without built-in web access: for example, "read the article at this link and summarize it."
items.install: claude mcp add fetch -- uvx mcp-server-fetch

items.name: Git MCP
items.description: A reference server for reading, searching, and operating on local git repositories: history, diffs, status, commits.
items.useFor: Developers whose tools lack built-in git: "show me what changed in this file over the last month" — the server runs git log and git diff for you.
items.install: claude mcp add git -- uvx mcp-server-git --repository /path/to/repository

items.name: Memory MCP
items.description: A reference server for persistent memory based on a knowledge graph: entities, relations, and observations are saved between sessions to a local file.
items.useFor: Anyone who wants Claude to remember facts across conversations: "remember that our staging lives at staging.example.com" — and it knows that in the next session.
items.install: claude mcp add memory -- npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-memory

items.name: Sequential Thinking MCP
items.description: A reference server for structured step-by-step reasoning: the model breaks a complex task into a chain of thoughts it can revise and branch.
items.useFor: For complex, multi-step planning and analysis tasks where seeing the line of reasoning helps: architectural decisions, untangling tricky bugs.
items.install: claude mcp add sequential-thinking -- npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking

items.name: Time MCP
items.description: A reference server for time and time zones: the current time in any zone and conversion between zones.
items.useFor: When you need precise time operations: "what time in London does a call scheduled for 9:00 New York time start?"
items.install: claude mcp add time -- uvx mcp-server-time

items.name: Everything MCP
items.description: An all-in-one demo server: demonstrates every protocol primitive — tools, resources, prompts. Not for production, but for learning and testing MCP clients.
items.useFor: Anyone writing their own MCP server or client: connect Everything and see what all the protocol's capabilities look like live.
items.install: claude mcp add everything -- npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-everything

items.name: GitHub MCP
items.description: The official GitHub server: repositories, issues, pull requests, code review, Actions. A hosted version exists — nothing to run locally.
items.useFor: Developers: "show me open PRs assigned to me," "create an issue for this bug," "why did CI fail on the last run" — right from Claude Code.
items.install: claude mcp add --transport http github https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp/ --header "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_GITHUB_PAT"

items.name: Playwright MCP
items.description: Microsoft's server: driving a real browser through Playwright's accessibility tree — navigation, clicks, forms, screenshots, without screenshot models.
items.useFor: For web automation and testing: "open our site, log in with a test account, and check that the dashboard loads."
items.install: claude mcp add playwright -- npx @playwright/mcp@latest

items.name: Chrome DevTools MCP
items.description: A server from the Chrome DevTools team (Google): gives the agent a live Chrome with access to the console, network, performance tracing, and debugging.
items.useFor: Frontend developers: "open the page, capture a performance trace, and tell me what's slow" or "what console errors show up in production."
items.install: claude mcp add chrome-devtools -- npx chrome-devtools-mcp@latest

items.name: Context7 MCP
items.description: Upstash's server: pulls up-to-date documentation and code examples for any library straight into the model's context — instead of stale knowledge from the training set.
items.useFor: Developers tired of hallucinated APIs: add "use context7" to your request — and Claude checks against the fresh docs for Next.js, Prisma, or any other library.
items.install: claude mcp add --transport http context7 https://mcp.context7.com/mcp

items.name: Supabase MCP
items.description: The official Supabase server: managing projects, tables, migrations, edge functions, and SQL queries through a remote MCP endpoint with OAuth.
items.useFor: Anyone building on Supabase: "create a profiles table with RLS policies" or "show me today's edge function logs."
items.install: claude mcp add --transport http supabase https://mcp.supabase.com/mcp

items.name: Sentry MCP
items.description: The official remote Sentry server: access to errors, stack traces, and releases from your monitoring systems. Authorization via OAuth with the /mcp command.
items.useFor: Teams with monitoring in Sentry: "what are the most frequent errors over the last day, and which deploy introduced them?"
items.install: claude mcp add --transport http sentry https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp

items.name: Stripe MCP
items.description: The official remote Stripe server: working with payments, customers, invoices, and the Stripe API docs from your assistant.
items.useFor: Anyone taking payments through Stripe: "create a $5 payment link" or "find a customer's recent failed charges."
items.install: claude mcp add --transport http stripe https://mcp.stripe.com

items.name: PayPal MCP
items.description: The official remote PayPal server: invoices, orders, subscriptions, and transaction reports through MCP.
items.useFor: Sellers on PayPal: "send a customer a $200 invoice" or "show me this week's transactions."
items.install: claude mcp add --transport http paypal https://mcp.paypal.com/mcp

items.name: HubSpot MCP
items.description: The official remote HubSpot CRM server: contacts, deals, companies, and tickets available to the assistant directly.
items.useFor: Sales managers: "find all deals in the negotiation stage worth over a million and put together a summary of each."
items.install: claude mcp add --transport http hubspot https://mcp.hubspot.com/anthropic

items.name: Notion MCP
items.description: The official remote Notion server with OAuth: searching, reading, and creating workspace pages and databases. Notion recommends the remote version over the local one.
items.useFor: Teams with a knowledge base in Notion: "find the page with the onboarding checklist and add an item about VPN access."
items.install: claude mcp add --transport http notion https://mcp.notion.com/mcp

items.name: Figma MCP
items.description: The official remote Figma server (Dev Mode MCP): hands the agent the structure of mockups, components, and design-system variables for accurate code generation from design.
items.useFor: Frontend developers and designers: "build this Figma frame into a React component with our design-system tokens."
items.install: claude mcp add --transport http figma https://mcp.figma.com/mcp

items.name: Cloudflare MCP
items.description: A suite of remote Cloudflare servers: documentation, observability (app logs and analytics), Workers Bindings, and more — over a dozen specialized endpoints.
items.useFor: Anyone deploying to Cloudflare Workers: "why is my worker returning a 500 — check the logs" (the observability server) or up-to-date API references (the docs server).
items.install: claude mcp add --transport http cloudflare-docs https://docs.mcp.cloudflare.com/mcp

items.name: Linear MCP
items.description: The official remote Linear server: searching, creating, and updating tasks, projects, and comments in the tracker. OAuth authorization via /mcp.
items.useFor: Teams on Linear: "create a task for this bug with the backend label and put it in the current cycle."
items.install: claude mcp add --transport http linear https://mcp.linear.app/mcp

items.name: Atlassian Rovo MCP (Jira, Confluence)
items.description: The official remote Atlassian server: Jira, Confluence, Jira Service Management, Bitbucket, and Compass through OAuth 2.1 or API tokens.
items.useFor: Teams on Jira/Confluence: "turn the decisions from this meeting into tickets" or "find the release process doc in Confluence and summarize it."
items.install: claude mcp add --transport http atlassian https://mcp.atlassian.com/v1/mcp/authv2

items.name: Asana MCP
items.description: The official remote Asana server (SSE transport): workspace tasks, projects, and statuses available to the assistant.
items.useFor: Teams on Asana: "show my tasks due this week and mark the finished ones as done."
items.install: claude mcp add --transport sse asana https://mcp.asana.com/sse

items.name: Vercel MCP
items.description: The official hosted Vercel server: managing projects and deployments, searching the docs. OAuth confirmation on every connection.
items.useFor: Anyone deploying to Vercel: "what's the status of the latest deployment and what's in the build logs?"
items.install: claude mcp add --transport http vercel https://mcp.vercel.com

items.name: Netlify MCP
items.description: The official Netlify server: creating and configuring sites, deployments, environment variables, and extensions — through natural language.
items.useFor: Anyone hosting on Netlify: "deploy the current branch to a preview and give me the link."
items.install: claude mcp add netlify -- npx -y @netlify/mcp

items.name: Hugging Face MCP
items.description: The official remote Hugging Face Hub server: searching models, datasets, Spaces, and papers, and running community tools on Gradio apps.
items.useFor: ML engineers and researchers: "find recent speech recognition models for English and compare their metrics."
items.install: claude mcp add --transport http hf-mcp-server https://huggingface.co/mcp

items.name: MongoDB MCP
items.description: The official MongoDB server: querying databases and collections, working with Atlas. The connection string is passed via an environment variable, and there's a read-only mode.
items.useFor: Developers with MongoDB: "how many orders in July and what's the average amount?" — without writing aggregations by hand.
items.install: claude mcp add mongodb --env MDB_MCP_CONNECTION_STRING="mongodb://localhost:27017/mydb" -- npx -y mongodb-mcp-server@latest --readOnly

items.name: DBHub (PostgreSQL, MySQL, and more)
items.description: A universal database server from Bytebase: PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, SQL Server, SQLite through a single DSN string. Used as the PostgreSQL example in the official Claude Code documentation.
items.useFor: Analysts and developers: connect a production replica as a read-only user and ask "what's the revenue for the month?" in plain language.
items.install: claude mcp add --transport stdio db -- npx -y @bytebase/dbhub --dsn "postgresql://readonly:pass@host:5432/analytics"

items.name: Firecrawl MCP
items.description: Firecrawl's server: scraping, crawling sites, and web search with clean markdown output. Available both as a local run and a hosted endpoint.
items.useFor: For collecting data from sites: "crawl a competitor's blog section and compile a list of topics for the year." Requires a Firecrawl API key.
items.install: claude mcp add firecrawl --env FIRECRAWL_API_KEY=fc-YOUR_KEY -- npx -y firecrawl-mcp

items.name: Brave Search MCP
items.description: The official Brave server: web search, image, video, news, and local place search through the Brave Search API. Requires an API key (a free tier is available).
items.useFor: When the assistant needs independent web search: "find recent reviews on this topic and give me links."
items.install: claude mcp add brave-search --env BRAVE_API_KEY=YOUR_KEY -- npx -y @brave/brave-search-mcp-server

items.name: The claude.ai Connector Catalog
items.description: Not a standalone server, but a way to connect without installing anything: hundreds of vetted connectors (each one an MCP server) can be added to claude.ai in a couple of clicks. Example categories: mail and calendar (Gmail, Google Calendar), documents (Google Drive, Notion), communication (Slack), task trackers (Linear, Asana, Jira), development tools (GitHub, Sentry).
items.useFor: claude.ai users without a terminal: open Settings → Connectors (or the catalog at claude.com/connectors), click "Connect," and authorize via OAuth — no installation required.
items.install: connect it as a connector on claude.ai: Settings → Connectors → Connect
